behavior_summary: This script implements event handlers for an east-west door that perform minimal local logic and delegate actual gameplay behavior to shared DOOR_NS routines.
On U8P_DOOR_EW::look (examine), the script prepares a per-door info/context, marks the door/process excluded from generic processing, and spawns DOOR_NS::00D0 to run the examine behavior.

On use (activate/open), the script prepares the same per-door info/context, pushes an action flag (0x01) to indicate the specific use action, marks the door excluded, and spawns DOOR_NS::08B4 to perform the open/activate behavior.

On gotHit, the script prepares per-door info/context, passes the hit parameter through, marks the door excluded, and spawns DOOR_NS::0C82 to resolve hit/damage effects (sound, damage, breakage, etc.).
Returned values from the spawned DOOR_NS routines are captured into a local temp and returned by the handler.
notable_control_flow: Each event handler follows the same control flow: push info id + info-pointer + door object -> call set info -> call process exclude -> optionally push event-specific args -> spawn the appropriate DOOR_NS routine -> store spawn retval and return it.
Handlers do not contain local decision logic for door state changes; they delegate to shared DOOR_NS code, making this script a thin adaptor that supplies per-instance metadata and flags.
The 'use' handler inserts an explicit byte (0x01) before spawning, indicating mode/flag dispatch to the shared handler.
open_questions: What exact fields and meanings exist in the indirect info/metadata structure the script pushes (the pointer pushed via the indirect 02h push)?
What exact behaviors DOOR_NS::00D0, DOOR_NS::08B4 and DOOR_NS::0C82 perform (sound, animation, locking, health/durability handling)?
What is the precise effect of process exclude in the engine for door objects (prevents generic stepping, visibility changes, registration differences)?

main_function.name: U8P_DOOR_EW::look
main_function.purpose: Prepare per-door metadata and delegate the examine/U8P_DOOR_EW::look event to a shared DOOR_NS handler (DOOR_NS::00D0).
entry_conditions: Event 0: an actor (usually the player) looks at or examines the door (trigger region/inspect action).

function_map.name: U8P_DOOR_EW::look
function_map.role: Examine/inspect handler that sets up door-specific metadata and delegates to DOOR_NS::00D0.

function_map.name: U8P_DOOR_EW::use
function_map.role: Use/activate handler that sets up metadata, pushes an action flag (0x01), and delegates to DOOR_NS::08B4.

function_map.name: U8P_DOOR_EW::gotHit
function_map.role: Hit/damage handler that forwards the hit value and metadata to DOOR_NS::0C82 for damage resolution.

external_calls.purpose: Populate per-door metadata/context (info id 0x0208 and the indirect info-pointer) before delegation.
Mark this door/process excluded from some generic engine processing prior to spawning the shared handler.
Delegate the examine/U8P_DOOR_EW::look behavior to shared DOOR_NS logic, passing the door object and prepared info/context.
Populate per-door metadata/context (info id 0x0207 and the indirect info-pointer) for use handling.
Exclude the door/process from generic processing prior to spawning the use handler.
Delegate the use/open behavior to DOOR_NS::08B4, passing an action flag (0x01), the door object, and prepared metadata.
Populate per-door metadata/context (info id 0x020C and the indirect info-pointer) before resolving the hit.
Exclude the door/process from generic processing prior to delegating hit resolution.
Delegate hit/damage resolution to shared DOOR_NS code, passing the hit value and prepared metadata.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements event handlers for an east-west door that perform minimal local logic and delegate actual gameplay behavior to shared DOOR_NS routines.
